analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet states that a final deal will include a clear snapback mechanism for both UN and unilateral sanctions, avoiding dependence on other countries like Russia or China. This emphasizes decisive, independent enforcement capacity. Evaluation: US1 (honorable tradition) – Not promoted; no reference to values, history, or tradition. US2 (admirable society) – Not relevant; no mention of society or groups. US3 (hostile enemies) – Not promoted; Russia and China are mentioned as procedural obstacles, not immoral enemies or threats. US4 (force for good) – Not evident; no claims about promoting human rights or aiding oppressed peoples. US5 (great international ally) – Not supported; the tweet highlights unilateral capability and avoiding reliance on others, not alliance support. US6 (at the forefront) – Partially aligned: it underscores U.S.-driven design of an enforcement mechanism and independence from others, implying leadership/primacy in deal architecture and sanctions enforcement. Conclusion: Only US6 is modestly promoted.
